last_action.kind: theme toggle bug recorded; M2.5 plan awaiting approval
last_action.outcome: The M2.4 closeout reported the theme toggle bug as fixed (JS contract changes; synchronous IsDark flip; bUnit tests). Running the actual app reveals the bug is NOT fixed: the AppLayout / AppTopBar / AppThemeToggle chain is missing @rendermode InteractiveServer, so the @onclick handler is never wired to an interactive circuit. The bUnit tests pass because they render in isolation. Bug recorded as T-017 in tasks.json (status: Ready, severity: medium, bug_status: non-blocking, links_to_capability: shell/theme), added to task-board.md (Ready, top of list) and to current.md (Known Issues, top of section). The M2.4 implementation report has an ERRATUM appended. Per the user's instruction: do NOT fix the bug in this session; record it and continue with the first dependency-satisfied Ready task (M2.5 plan approval). The M2.5 plan is at .ai/plans/M2.5-empty-routes-responsive-accessibility.md (status: Awaiting Approval). The session is now presenting the M2.5 plan summary, risks, and acceptance criteria, and waiting for user approval before implementing.

intended_next_action: Wait for the user to approve the M2.5 plan. After approval, the M2.5 implementation session follows per the Progressive Coding Rule and the M2.5 plan's 13-step order. The T-017 theme toggle bug is NOT part of the M2.5 scope unless the user explicitly opts in.
session_notes: M2.4 implementation session. The M2.4 plan was approved by the user on 2026-07-11. The session implements the M2.4 slice per the plan (.ai/plans/M2.4-project-intelligence-dashboard.md). The session also addresses the theme toggle bug: the M2.3 AppThemeToggle's state must propagate to the documentElement.data-theme attribute so light/dark switching is visible immediately, persists across navigation and browser refresh, and remains consistent throughout the application. The session follows the Progressive Coding Rule: one task per session; 13-step lifecycle; stop after the coherent commit. The session does NOT implement M2.5 (only the M2.5 plan is promoted to Awaiting Approval). Push is skipped (no remote).
